TeoNAM STAM — GWAS Manhattan with candidate genes (Chen 2019 Fig 4C) [118K panel]

Empirical-truth validation on the authentic 118,838-SNP Chen 2019 GWAS panel

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 7, 2026

What this is. The STAM GWAS Manhattan (Chen 2019 Fig 4C) reproduced on the TeoNAM genotypes, with the domestication candidate genes placed at their real B73 v5 coordinates (MaizeGDB genemodel_locus → b73_gene_coords), a red star at each gene’s genomic position, and the LOD = 5 dotted significance line.

Important118K-panel edition

This is the -118k edition: every figure and table is regenerated on the authentic 118,838-SNP Chen 2019 GWAS panel (Qiuyue Chen’s Drive release, lifted AGPv2→v5), not the earlier 51K map-panel approximation. Data paths point to the *_118k* artifacts (GWAS scan stam_gwas_scan_118k.csv; the four stam_gwas_<caller>_118k_sweep.csv; mlm_loco_scan_lb01_118k.csv; 118K het-fraction tables). The JLM QTL-recovery arm (Fig 4A lollipop, candidate-overlap) deliberately keeps its own inputs — JLM runs on the 6K JLM panel, a separate reproduction unaffected by the GWAS marker set.

Caveat — prose vs figures: the interpretive text was written against the 51K sweep; specific inline numbers (e.g. tb1-peak −log10P, chr3-ghost excesses) may lag the regenerated 118K figures/tables shown here, which are authoritative. A prose-reconciliation pass is a follow-up.

This notebook loads pre-computed CSVs and plots (compute lives in the scripts/ directory): the per-marker GWAS scans (teonam_stam_gwas_scan.R interpolated / teonam_stam_gwas_family_imputed.R family-imputed) and the candidate-gene overlap (teonam_candidate_overlap.R). We show both the family-imputed scan (natural scatter) and the interpolated/densified scan (block-terraced — the artifact of step-interpolated genotypes, see below).

library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
# Coordinate transformer replicating fastman_gg's internal x layout, so we can
# place gene labels / lines at arbitrary (chr, bp). From inv4m make_manhattan_plots.Rmd.
get_transformer <- function(m) {
  cmat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(
    split(m[order(m$CHR, m$BP), ], m$CHR[order(m$CHR, m$BP)]),
    function(d) {
      data.frame(
        CHR = d$CHR[1], min_bp = min(d$BP),
        width = max(d$BP) - min(d$BP),
        medgap = if (nrow(d) > 1) median(diff(sort(d$BP))) else 1
      )
    }
  ))
  cmat <- cmat[order(cmat$CHR), ]
  maxgap <- max(cmat$medgap, na.rm = TRUE)
  numc <- nrow(cmat)
  cmat$base <- 0
  cmat$midp <- 0
  cmat$midp[1] <- cmat$width[1] / 2
  for (i in 2:numc) {
    cmat$base[i] <- cmat$base[i - 1] + cmat$width[i - 1] + maxgap
    cmat$midp[i] <- cmat$base[i] + cmat$width[i] / 2
  }
  fac <- numc / cmat$midp[numc]
  cmat$basef <- fac * cmat$base
  function(chr, bp) {
    i <- match(chr, cmat$CHR)
    fac * (bp - cmat$min_bp[i]) + cmat$basef[i]
  }
}

LOD <- 5 # Chen 2019: P < 1e-5 (LOD = 5)

#' STAM GWAS Manhattan with candidate genes at their true v5 positions.
#' Odd chromosomes black, even gray75; y-axis expanded one major-tick unit above
#' the top tick; gene labels repelled into the headroom above the maximum peak
#' (segments back to the red star at each gene's local peak).
#' @param scan_csv per-marker scan (SNP, CHR, BP[v5], P)
#' @param title    plot title
#' @param out_png  optional path to save a PNG
plot_stam_manhattan <- function(scan_csv, title, out_png = NULL, mark = NULL) {
  scan <- if (is.data.frame(scan_csv)) as.data.frame(scan_csv) else as.data.frame(fread(scan_csv))
  scan <- scan[is.finite(scan$P) & scan$P > 0, ]
  m <- scan[order(scan$CHR, scan$BP), c("SNP", "CHR", "BP", "P")]

  ov <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_candidate_overlap.csv")) # symbol, chr, start (v5)
  # star height = local max -log10P within +/-500 kb IN THIS scan
  ov[, y := vapply(seq_len(.N), function(i) {
    w <- m$P[m$CHR == chr[i] & abs(m$BP - start[i]) <= 5e5]
    if (length(w)) -log10(min(w)) else NA_real_
  }, numeric(1))]
  tr <- get_transformer(m)
  ov[, BPn := tr(chr, start)]
  ov <- ov[is.finite(y)]

  mp <- -log10(min(m$P)) # maximum peak
  ticks <- pretty(c(0, mp))
  unit <- diff(ticks)[1]
  ytop <- max(ticks) + unit # one major-tick unit of headroom

  p <- fastman_gg(
    m = m, snp = "SNP", col = c("black", "gray75"), maxP = mp,
    genomewideline = NULL, suggestiveline = NULL,
    ylab = expression(-log[10](italic(P))), xlab = ""
  ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = LOD, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7, color = "black") +
    geom_point(
      data = ov, aes(x = BPn, y = y), inherit.aes = FALSE,
      shape = 8, size = 3, stroke = 1, color = "red"
    ) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = ov, aes(x = BPn, y = y, label = symbol),
      inherit.aes = FALSE, fontface = "italic", size = 5,
      segment.color = "grey50", min.segment.length = 0, box.padding = 0.5,
      max.overlaps = Inf, ylim = c(mp + 0.3 * unit, ytop)
    ) + # above the max peak
    ggtitle(title) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = ticks, limits = c(0, ytop),
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0))
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 1, size = 16, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2)
    )

  if (!is.null(mark)) { # dashed marker at an arbitrary locus (e.g. the chr3 ghost)
    xm <- tr(mark$chr, mark$bp)
    p <- p + geom_vline(xintercept = xm, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5)
  }
  if (!is.null(out_png)) ggsave(out_png, p, width = 9, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  p
}

#' Chen 2019 Fig 4A: JLM QTL genomic distribution as a lollipop plot.
#' Full 10-chromosome axis is obtained by passing the dense scan to fastman_gg
#' with fully transparent points (col = transparent); the JLM QTL are overlaid as
#' blue stems + points, candidate genes labeled bold-italic above.
#' @param jlm_txt  TASSEL StepwiseOLSModelFitter output (stam_jlm_native1.txt): Name,
#'   Locus, Position (v5 bp), `pr>F`
#' @param scan_csv dense per-marker scan, only used (transparent) to lay out the axis
plot_stam_lollipop <- function(jlm_txt, scan_csv, title, out_png = NULL) {
  scan <- as.data.frame(fread(scan_csv))
  scan <- scan[is.finite(scan$P) & scan$P > 0, ]
  maxis <- scan[order(scan$CHR, scan$BP), c("SNP", "CHR", "BP", "P")]

  j <- fread(jlm_txt)
  j <- j[!(Name %in% c("mean", "Family", "Error"))]
  q <- data.table(
    SNP = j$Name, CHR = as.integer(j$Locus), BP = as.integer(j$Position),
    logP = -log10(as.numeric(j$`pr>F`))
  )
  q <- q[logP >= LOD] # keep only QTL significant at LOD 5 (-log10P >= 5)
  ov <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_candidate_overlap.csv")) # symbol, chr, start (v5)
  ov <- ov[symbol %in% c("ts2", "tb1", "tru1", "zmm16", "tsh4")] # JLM Fig-4A candidates (an1 is GWAS-only)
  q[, gene := ""] # assign each candidate gene to its NEAREST JLM QTL (1:1)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(ov))) {
    idx <- which(q$CHR == ov$chr[i])
    if (length(idx)) {
      k <- idx[which.min(abs(q$BP[idx] - ov$start[i]))]
      q$gene[k] <- ov$symbol[i]
    }
  }
  tr <- get_transformer(maxis)
  q[, BPn := tr(CHR, BP)]

  maxlogP <- max(q$logP)
  brks <- seq(0, floor(maxlogP / 10) * 10, by = 10)
  ytop <- maxlogP * 1.18 # headroom for the top label

  p <- fastman_gg(
    m = maxis, snp = "SNP", col = c("#00000000", "#00000000"),
    maxP = maxlogP, genomewideline = NULL, suggestiveline = NULL,
    ylab = expression(-log[10](italic(P))), xlab = ""
  ) +
    geom_segment(
      data = q, aes(x = BPn, xend = BPn, y = 0, yend = logP),
      inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "blue", linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = q, aes(x = BPn, y = logP), inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "blue", size = 2.5
    ) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = q[gene != ""], aes(x = BPn, y = logP, label = gene),
      inherit.aes = FALSE, fontface = "bold.italic", size = 5,
      segment.color = NA, box.padding = 0.4, point.padding = 0.3,
      nudge_y = 0.05 * maxlogP, seed = 1, max.overlaps = Inf
    ) +
    ggtitle(title) + theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = brks, limits = c(0, ytop), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0))) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 1, size = 16, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2)
    )
  if (!is.null(out_png)) ggsave(out_png, p, width = 9, height = 4, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
  p
}

#' Coverage-sweep line-Manhattan (geom_line, one line per coverage).
#' −log10P vs genomic position along the union grid; `group = interaction(CHR,
#' coverage)` so lines never connect across chromosomes. Color = coverage on a
#' viridis ramp, the λ = ∞ truth baseline in black. LOD = 5 dotted. NO gene labels.
#' Reused for the control / nNIL / RTIGER panels of the degradation composite.
#' @param sweep_csv combined sweep CSV (SNP, CHR, BP[v5], P, coverage; coverage=Inf
#'   for the λ=∞ baseline rows)
#' @param title panel title (markdown)
#' @param legend show the coverage legend (FALSE for composite panels sharing one)
#' @param base_size theme base size
plot_sweep_line <- function(sweep_csv, title, legend = TRUE, base_size = 14) {
  sw <- as.data.table(fread(sweep_csv))
  sw <- sw[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  sw[, logP := -log10(P)]
  # x layout from the union grid (every coverage carries the identical marker set)
  tr <- get_transformer(as.data.frame(sw[coverage == sw$coverage[1], .(CHR, BP)]))
  sw[, BPn := tr(CHR, BP)]

  # Legend order: infinity (truth) at top, then coverage in descending order.
  cov_levels <- c("∞", "20", "10", "5", "1", "0.5", "0.2", "0.1")
  sw[, cov_lab := factor(ifelse(is.finite(coverage), formatC(coverage), "∞"),
    levels = cov_levels
  )]
  # Colour mapping is fixed by coverage value (0.1 = darkest viridis ... 20 =
  # lightest; ∞ = black), independent of the legend order above.
  cov_asc <- c("0.1", "0.2", "0.5", "1", "5", "10", "20")
  pal <- setNames(grDevices::hcl.colors(7, "viridis"), cov_asc)
  pal["∞"] <- "black"
  axis_df <- sw[, .(mid = (min(BPn) + max(BPn)) / 2), by = CHR][order(CHR)]
  ytop <- 20 # shared y-limit across all panels for cross-method comparison

  # Draw order (z-stack): 20x first, then descending to 0.1x, and ∞ LAST so the
  # darker low-coverage / black-truth profiles sit on top. dord = -coverage sorts
  # finite coverage descending (20 -> -20 first); ∞ -> Inf sorts last.
  sw[, dord := ifelse(is.finite(coverage), -coverage, Inf)]

  ggplot(
    sw[order(dord, CHR, BP)],
    aes(x = BPn, y = logP, color = cov_lab, group = interaction(CHR, coverage))
  ) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = LOD, linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.7, color = "grey30") +
    scale_color_manual(values = pal, name = "coverage (×)", drop = FALSE) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = axis_df$mid, labels = axis_df$CHR, expand = c(0.01, 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, ytop), expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0))) +
    labs(x = "chromosome", y = expression(-log[10](italic(P))), title = title) +
    theme_classic(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0, size = base_size, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.2),
      # legend INSIDE the plotting area, top-right corner (top panel only)
      legend.position = if (legend) c(0.995, 0.97) else "none",
      legend.justification = c(1, 1),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.7), colour = NA),
      legend.key.size = unit(0.85, "lines"),
      legend.title = element_text(size = base_size - 3),
      legend.text = element_text(size = base_size - 4),
      legend.margin = margin(1, 3, 1, 3)
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.2)))
}

Candidate genes: closest marker vs most-significant marker (v5)

For each STAM candidate gene (B73 v5), two different “nearby marker” choices from the pre-JLM marker set (the 51,004-marker family-imputed GWAS scan): the closest marker (smallest bp distance to the gene body) and the window-max marker (highest −log₁₀P within ±500 kb — a 1 Mb window centered on the gene). They are not the same marker at any gene: the closest marker sits essentially on the gene but carries little signal, while the significant local signal is a nearby but distinct marker. This is the reference for what “near the gene” means in the Fig 4A lollipop below (which labels each gene onto its nearest JLM QTL).

sc <- fread(here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_118k.csv"))
sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
cand <- c(
  ts2 = "STAM1.1", tb1 = "STAM1.2", tru1 = "STAM3.1",
  zmm16 = "STAM3.2", tsh4 = "STAM7.1", an1 = "GWAS(chr1)"
)
sym <- fread(here("data/ref/maize_v5_gene_symbols.tsv"))
setnames(sym, c("gene_id", "symbol"))
coord <- fread(here("data/ref/b73_gene_coords.tsv"),
  header = FALSE,
  col.names = c("gene_id", "chr", "start", "end")
)
gg <- merge(sym[symbol %in% names(cand)], coord, by = "gene_id")
gg[, chr := as.integer(sub("^chr", "", chr))][, qtl := cand[symbol]]
WIN <- 5e5 # +/-500 kb around the gene body (1 Mb window)
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d) # closest to gene body
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- which.max(w$logP) # max signal in window
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], QTL = gg$qtl[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max Δstart (bp)` = w$BP[kw] - s, `window-max -log10P` = round(w$logP[kw], 2)
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab, format.args = list(big.mark = ","), caption = paste(
  "STAM candidate genes (B73 v5): the CLOSEST marker vs the highest -log10P marker",
  "within +/-500 kb (authentic 118K GWAS scan, 118,514 markers). Δstart = marker bp -",
  "gene start (v5), signed (negative = upstream); the two markers differ at every gene."
))
STAM candidate genes (B73 v5): the CLOSEST marker vs the highest -log10P marker within +/-500 kb (authentic 118K GWAS scan, 118,514 markers). Δstart = marker bp - gene start (v5), signed (negative = upstream); the two markers differ at every gene.
gene QTL chr gene start (v5) closest Δstart (bp) closest -log10P window-max Δstart (bp) window-max -log10P
ts2 STAM1.1 1 46,511,946 1,592 3.30 59,560 11.62
an1 GWAS(chr1) 1 246,895,757 656 7.29 -368,239 12.14
tb1 STAM1.2 1 272,330,564 -92,455 4.76 -263,817 19.84
tru1 STAM3.1 3 152,086,191 420 6.77 149,536 14.18
zmm16 STAM3.2 3 176,074,772 1,564 13.64 -401,160 15.44
tsh4 STAM7.1 7 138,540,580 372 5.74 -117,306 7.94

JLM QTL genomic distribution (Fig 4A)

The STAM QTL detected by JLM as a lollipop plot across all 10 chromosomes (height = −log10 P from TASSEL’s stepwise model), candidate genes bold-italic above the corresponding QTL. Reproduces Chen 2019 Fig 4A.

p_lolli <- plot_stam_lollipop(
  here("data/teonam/tassel/stam_jlm_native1.txt"),
  here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_118k.csv"),
  "STAM — JLM QTL genomic distribution (TeoNAM)",
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_jlm_lollipop_118k.png")
)
p_lolli

Authentic 118K panel (the faithful Fig 4C)

Chen 2019’s directly-typed/imputed 118,838-SNP GWAS HapMap — natural GWAS scatter.

p_unimp <- plot_stam_manhattan(
  here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_118k.csv"),
  "STAM — GWAS (TeoNAM, authentic 118K panel), candidate genes at v5 positions",
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_manhattan_annotated_118k.png")
)
p_unimp

(Interpolation-artifact panel — N/A for the authentic panel)

On the 51K map panel this section documented the step-interpolation terraced-plateau artifact (cross-family gaps densified). The authentic 118K HapMap is directly imputed by Chen’s pipeline — there is no cross-family step-interpolation, so no terracing artifact applies. This panel therefore re-plots the same authentic 118K scan (identical to the panel above); it is retained only so the internal composite below keeps its three-panel layout. Not a distinct result.

p_interp <- plot_stam_manhattan(
  here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_118k.csv"),
  "STAM — GWAS (TeoNAM, authentic 118K panel; = panel above)",
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_manhattan_118k_annotated.png")
)
p_interp

Composite (Fig 4A lollipop + Fig 4C family-imputed + interpolated)

composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  p_lolli, p_unimp, p_interp,
  ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr"
)
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_composite_118k.png"), composite,
  width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
composite

RTIGER ancestry vs sequencing coverage (degradation sweep)

The panel-C baseline above is the λ = ∞, perfect-caller curve (GWAS on the interpolated truth genotypes). All sweeps below run on the same full 51,004-marker GWAS grid as the Fig-4C panels above. Here we ask how low-coverage sequencing degrades that signal through the RTIGER ancestry caller: for each family × coverage λ ∈ {0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20}× we simulate reads from the real genotypes (.draw_counts, pi_floor=0, k_decay=1, error=0.01), call ancestry with call_ancestry(caller = "rtiger", rigidity = 2) (the calibrated rigidity_star), step-interpolate the recovered block onto the same union grid, and rerun the same STAM ~ Family + marker scan. Compute lives in scripts/teonam_rtiger_sweep.R --generate; this chunk only loads the combined sweep CSV and plots.

The read as a line-Manhattan (geom_line, one line per coverage; chromosomes never connect because group = interaction(CHR, coverage)): coverage on a viridis ramp, the λ = ∞ truth baseline in black as the reference curve, LOD = 5 dotted. The striking result is how robust RTIGER is — ancestry is a long-block signal, so even 0.1× (~9.5 % of markers carrying a read) reconstructs the blocks well enough that the tb1 peak only slips from ≈17.3 (baseline) to ≈16.1. Degradation is real but lives mostly in HET recovery (het-recall 0.77 at 0.1× → 1.00 at ≥20×), not in the additive QTL peaks.

p_sweep <- plot_sweep_line(
  here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_118k_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — RTIGER ancestry, GWAS −log10P vs coverage",
  base_size = 15
)
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_118k_sweep_manhattan.png"),
  p_sweep,
  width = 9, height = 4.6, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
p_sweep

Coverage-degradation composite: interpolation control / nNIL / RTIGER / LB-Impute

The four-panel contrast (agent/teonam-control-sweep-plan.md). All four degrade the identical truth mosaics over the same coverage grid λ ∈ {0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 20}× (+ the λ = ∞ interpolated-truth baseline) and feed the same STAM ~ Family + marker GWAS — only the genotype caller differs:

  • A — interpolation control (nilHMM::call_gl(prior = "hwe") + per-RIL step-interpolation; scripts/teonam_control_sweep.R): the deliberately het-prone no-HMM caller — a single ALT read is called HET via the 2p(1−p) HWE prior term.
  • B — nNIL (call_ancestry(caller = "nnil", rrate = rrate_star, err = 0.01), f_1 = 0.08, f_2 = 0.15; scripts/teonam_nnil_sweep.R): count emission + geometric duration.
  • C — RTIGER (caller = "rtiger", rigidity = 2; scripts/teonam_rtiger_sweep.R): count emission + rigidity duration.
  • D — LB-Impute (call_ancestry(caller = "lbimpute", unit = "cm", recombdist = recombdist_star ≈ 5.5); scripts/teonam_lbimpute_sweep.R): coverage-aware emission + distance-dependent (map-aware cM) transition with a double-recombination penalty, full-chromosome Viterbi (nilHMM native port of Fragoso et al. 2014). recombdist is the donor-fragment-Dice-optimal recombdist_star from the BC1S4 calibration (scripts/02_calibrate.R), read from calib_params.csv — not hardcoded.

The contrast is het handling. The three duration/distance-aware HMMs (B, C, D) tie neighbouring markers into ancestry blocks, so a lone ALT read cannot open a het call — het-call fraction stays near the ~8 % truth level and the STAM peaks hold near the baseline down to 0.1×. LB-Impute (D), at the calibrated recombdist_star ≈ 5.5 cM, degrades gracefully like B and C rather than over-smoothing: its tb1 peak holds near the baseline across the grid (≈16.3 at 0.1× vs ≈17.3 baseline), never over-shooting. (The tighter calibrated distance scale allows more transitions than the earlier hardcoded recombdist = 50, which had produced over-long imputed homozygous blocks and an over-shot peak.) The control (A) decides each site independently, so at low coverage the HWE prior inflates het to ~20 % (het excess) and the tb1 peak degrades the other way (≈13.4 at 0.1× vs ≈17.3 baseline). geom_line, colored by coverage, no gene labels, LOD = 5, chromosomes never connect (group = interaction(CHR, coverage)).

A pericentromeric block-imputation “ghost” on chromosome 3 at 0.1×. At the lowest coverage a spurious peak appears near the middle of chr3 (≈110–115 Mb, on the chromosome axis label) — proximal to both chr3 STAM candidates, tru1 (STAM3.1, ≈152 Mb) and zmm16 (STAM3.2, ≈176 Mb), which sit in the right half of the chromosome. It is a false positive: the λ = ∞ baseline has no peak there (≈8.5), whereas the 0.1× line rises to ≈12.4 (excess ≈+3.9 over baseline). The ghost sits on the proximal shoulder of the chr3 pericentromere — the restricted-recombination zone reported at ≈80–90 Mb in B73 v5 (pan-genome cenhap boundaries) and independently visible in this study’s own native cM map, where the recombination rate collapses to ≈0.06 cM/Mb across ≈80–100 Mb (~40× below the ≈2 cM/Mb near the telomeres). Recombination is so suppressed there that cM barely advances across tens of Mb, so with the map-aware cM transition adjacent markers are separated by ~zero cM and the state stays highly persistent; at 0.1× (only ~9.5 % of markers carrying a read) LB-Impute lays down a long spurious homozygous block through this low-recombination valley that tracks the phenotype. It is a block-caller effect: the no-HMM interpolation control (A) shows no central ghost (excess ≈0), nNIL (B) and RTIGER (C) show milder versions (excess ≈+2.3–2.6), and LB-Impute (D) is the most affected (≈+3.9).

Note this ghost is a genuine consequence of the calibrated recombdist_star (≈5.5 cM): with the earlier hardcoded recombdist = 50 the chr3 middle stayed near baseline (excess ≈+1) and the over-imputation instead landed on the right, inflating the real zmm16 region (≈16.7 vs ≈14.8 baseline). The tighter calibrated distance scale represents the real peaks more faithfully but, interacting with pericentromeric cM compression that the BC1S4 calibration sim does not model, produces this central false block at extreme low coverage. Treat the 0.1× chr3-mid peak as a coverage-limit artifact, not signal.

het_ctrl <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_control_het_fraction_118k.csv"))[, .(coverage, control = round(het_frac, 3))]
het_nnil <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_nnil_het_fraction_118k.csv"))[, .(coverage, nnil = round(het_frac, 3))]
het_tab <- merge(het_ctrl, het_nnil, by = "coverage")
het_tab[] # HET-call fraction vs coverage: control (het excess at low λ) vs nNIL (flat ~truth)
Key: <coverage>
   coverage control  nnil
      <num>   <num> <num>
1:      0.1   0.117 0.222
2:      0.2   0.117 0.224
3:      0.5   0.119 0.221
4:      1.0   0.119 0.210
5:      5.0   0.072 0.098
6:     10.0   0.053 0.063
7:     20.0   0.049 0.053
# Legend lives INSIDE the top panel (A), top-right; the rest carry none.
pA <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_control_118k_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — interpolation control (GL + HWE)",
  legend = TRUE
)
pB <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_nnil_118k_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — nNIL ancestry",
  legend = FALSE
)
pC <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_rtiger_118k_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — RTIGER ancestry",
  legend = FALSE
)
pD <- plot_sweep_line(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_lbimpute_118k_sweep.csv"),
  "STAM — LB-Impute ancestry (map-aware cM)",
  legend = FALSE
)

composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(pA, pB, pC, pD,
  ncol = 1, align = "v", axis = "lr",
  labels = c("A", "B", "C", "D"), label_size = 18
)
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_sweep_composite_118k.png"), composite,
  width = 9, height = 16, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
composite

Is the chr3-mid ghost a real QTL? Structure/kinship control at 0.1× (MLM Q + K, LOCO)

The composite above is an OLS scan (STAM ~ Family + marker), which models neither kinship nor fine population structure — exactly what inflates broad signals in low-recombination regions. Chen 2019’s GWAS instead used a mixed model with Q = 5 PCs + K = kinship. To test whether the 0.1× chr3-pericentromere ghost is a real QTL or a structure/LD confound, we re-scan the same 0.1× LB-Impute genotypes three ways (compute in scripts/teonam_mlm_loco_lb01_118k.R — an EMMAX port: VanRaden K + 5 PCs + REML δ + P3D GLS; genotype matrix from scripts/teonam_lbimpute_sweep_118k.R --generate --save-matrix):

  • A — OLS (Family): the composite’s model. Ghost present (chr3-mid ≈12.4).
  • B — MLM (Q + K): kinship + structure controlled (λ_GC = 1.10). The ghost collapses to ≈4.7, back below the LOD-5 line — the signature of a structure/LD confound, not a QTL. (The real tru1/zmm16 peaks are pulled down here too — the known MLM proximal over-correction on their own chromosome.)
  • C — LOCO: kinship built excluding the tested chromosome. The real QTL return (tru1/159/zmm16 ≈ 9.6–12.6) — but so does the ghost (≈10.7), because dropping chr3 from the kinship removes the correction for its own pericentromeric structure. At 0.1× the ghost becomes indistinguishable from a real QTL under LOCO.

Red dashed line = the chr3 pericentromere ghost (~112 Mb); the tru1/zmm16 stars mark the real distal QTL. Takeaway: the ghost is a structure/LD confound that full-K MLM controls even at 0.1×, but low-coverage block imputation inflates it enough to nearly survive Q + K and to fully resurface under LOCO — so LOCO peaks in low-recombination regions cannot be trusted at low coverage.

lb01 <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_gwas_lbimpute_118k_lambda0.1.csv")) # OLS, Family only
ml <- fread(here("results/sim/teonam/mlm_loco_scan_lb01_118k.csv")) # MLM Q+K (p_full) + LOCO (p_loco)

ols_s <- lb01[is.finite(P) & P > 0, .(SNP, CHR, BP, P)]
full_s <- ml[is.finite(p_full) & p_full > 0, .(SNP = marker, CHR = chr, BP = bp, P = p_full)]
loco_s <- ml[is.finite(p_loco) & p_loco > 0, .(SNP = marker, CHR = chr, BP = bp, P = p_loco)]

ghost <- list(chr = 3, bp = 112e6) # chr3 pericentromere ghost locus
pO <- plot_stam_manhattan(ols_s, "STAM — LB-Impute 0.1×, OLS (Family)", mark = ghost)
pF <- plot_stam_manhattan(full_s, "STAM — LB-Impute 0.1×, MLM (Q + K)", mark = ghost)
pL <- plot_stam_manhattan(loco_s, "STAM — LB-Impute 0.1×, LOCO", mark = ghost)

ghost_grid <- cowplot::plot_grid(pO, pF, pL,
  ncol = 1, align = "v", axis = "lr",
  labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 18
)
ggsave(here("results/sim/teonam/stam_ghost_mlm_loco_118k.png"), ghost_grid,
  width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white"
)
ghost_grid
regmax <- function(dt, pcol, lo, hi) {
  z <- dt[chr == 3 & bp >= lo * 1e6 & bp <= hi * 1e6]
  pv <- z[[pcol]][is.finite(z[[pcol]]) & z[[pcol]] > 0]
  if (length(pv)) round(max(-log10(pv)), 1) else NA_real_
}
olsdt <- copy(lb01)
setnames(olsdt, c("CHR", "BP", "P"), c("chr", "bp", "p_ols"))
gtab <- data.table(
  region = c("pericentromere 80–120 (ghost)", "tru1 ~152", "159-mid", "zmm16 ~176"),
  lo = c(80, 150, 157, 174), hi = c(120, 154, 162, 178)
)
gtab[, `:=`(
  OLS = mapply(function(a, b) regmax(olsdt, "p_ols", a, b), lo, hi),
  `MLM (Q+K)` = mapply(function(a, b) regmax(ml, "p_full", a, b), lo, hi),
  LOCO = mapply(function(a, b) regmax(ml, "p_loco", a, b), lo, hi)
)]
gtab[, .(region, OLS, `MLM (Q+K)`, LOCO)] # chr3 max -log10P at 0.1x; LOD = 5
                          region   OLS MLM (Q+K)  LOCO
                          <char> <num>     <num> <num>
1: pericentromere 80–120 (ghost)   8.8       1.3   6.0
2:                     tru1 ~152  13.3       6.9  15.8
3:                       159-mid  14.2       6.3  13.8
4:                    zmm16 ~176  14.3       4.4  12.2
